item.title: Explanation.
item.content:encoded:
It's/was a Pattern Screamer, something that desperately wants to exist.

This Pattern Screamer is a thought form, an idea, if you will (Read the S&C Plastics tales to get a better grasp of it), meaning it is what the majority of sapient beings think it is.
Why a goat lady?
Some hiker found something (completely unrelated since the scp didn't exist yet) in the woods, came home and spread the story.
The Screamer, seeing the opportunity, leeched onto the story, becoming one with it.
People went to investigate, found it, thus anchoring it to reality.

Enter Foundation.

The article specifies the "Disinformation Campaign "Goat Woman" was mobilized and distributed to local media outlets while local law enforcement were issued an amnestic regimen." now this means that everyone either forgot about it, or thinks its a goat woman, thus, it is one.

The entirety of the containment procedures is about feeding that idea to people so it stays controlled.

The skip changed over time because stories are misinterpreted and/or retold differently over time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
According to previous articles in the wiki, if a person touches a pattern-screamer, they never did/do/will exist.

Since the researchers who made contact with it never existed, no one has ever made contact with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"SCP-4782: Holy shit, this lady is a-"

forgive me if I'm being dense but wot?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this one, one of my favorite SCPs, engaging, fun, and at times funny while still being unsettling. especially when the pieces all fall into place w that pattern screamer tag.

just one minor quibble:

description: has remained motionless for the duration of its containment.

addendum (blurb before Breen interview): SCP-4782 is walking in place.

those two seem to conflict with one another. even if its "walking in place,", that's different from motionless imo. I hope I'm not being insufferably pedantic or anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i don't get it, it's just some goat woman, why does the foundation act like it's a huge thr—

`sees tag pattern-screamer`

oh.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a void space as referenced in a couple of pattern screamer articles that reacts based on what your mind tries to make sense of it. There is no actual goat person here, researchers just expect to see this so they do.

No one has ever physically interacted with it because you can't interact with nothing. Also the shit that goes on in djkaktus pattern screamers article

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Little confusing to me or maybe I'm stupid. I kinda like the idea from what I got but I'd like for it to be clearer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this whole article leaves me wondering "what is going on here?"

it's a fun mystery. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I got it correctly, I think it's too similar to that one chair. It was still a good read though.
